docstring:
/**
 * @brief Writes a newline character to the stream and flushes it.
 *
 * Standard library manipulator equivalent to `std::endl` for `char` streams.
 * Inserts the line-terminator into @p os and then calls `os.flush()` so that
 * any buffered output is sent to the underlying device.
 *
 * The newline character is obtained by widening `'\n'` through the stream's
 * cached `std::ctype<char>` facet (read from the `ios_base` subobject reached
 * via the vtable's offset-to-top, at offset 0xF0). A fast path is taken when
 * the facet already has its widen table built (byte at offset 0x38 is
 * non-zero) or when the facet's `do_widen` slot still points at the default
 * `std::ctype<char>::do_widen`; in both cases the resulting character is
 * simply `'\n'`. Otherwise `_M_widen_init()` is invoked and the facet's
 * virtual `do_widen('\n')` is called to produce the locale-specific newline.
 *
 * @param os The output stream to write the newline to and then flush.
 *
 * @return Reference to @p os (the value returned by `os.flush()`), enabling
 *         chaining with further `operator<<` calls.
 *
 * @note If the cached ctype facet pointer is null, this function calls
 *       `std::__throw_bad_cast()` and does not return normally.
 * @note Unlike writing `'\n'` directly, this manipulator forces a flush of
 *       the stream buffer, which can be expensive in tight output loops.
 *
 * @code
 *     std::cout << "hello world" << std::endl;
 * @endcode
 */
assumptions: Identified the function as std::endl<char> based on the mangled name in the symbol; treated it as a wrapper around ostream::put('\n') followed by flush().
Interpreted the load at [vtable-0x18] as the standard Itanium-ABI offset-to-top used to reach the ios_base subobject of basic_ostream.
Interpreted the pointer at ios_base+0xf0 as the cached ctype<char> facet (libstdc++ layout) and the byte at facet+0x38 as the _M_widen_ok flag.

Interpreted sub_12b0 (called when the facet pointer is null) as std::__throw_bad_cast, sub_1290 as ctype<char>::_M_widen_init, and sub_11b0 as basic_ostream::put — these match the standard libstdc++ inlined endl pattern.

The compare of the vtable slot at +0x30 against `expected` was inferred to be the devirtualisation guard checking whether do_widen has been overridden; the default branch skips the virtual call and uses '\n' directly.
Final tail call returns through ostream::flush; the trailing `return arg1` in the pseudocode is just the ABI return-value register holding the ostream reference returned by flush.
Dropped optimizer artefacts: the explicit prologue/epilogue (push/pop, rsp adjustment), the duplicated tail blocks for the two widen paths, and the `movsx` sign-extension of the resulting char.
String-literal table entries (ELF_MAGIC_STR, LD_LINUX_X86_64_PATH, etc.) are unrelated rodata and were ignored — none are referenced by this function.
divergences: [low] control_flow: Original pseudocode jumps to L_19a3 (calling 0x12b0) when facet is null, then falls through with `nop; nop; return arg1;`. The rewrite calls __throw_bad_cast but does not mark it [[noreturn]] explicitly; behaviour matches since the call is to a no-return function.
[low] other: Rewrite uses [-3] (i.e. -0x18 for 8-byte ptrdiff_t) which matches the original `*&[ret-0x18]`. Equivalent.
[low] other: Original reads at [var0+0x38] and tests against zero with the same semantics; widths match (byte flag).
[low] dropped_call: Fast path in pseudocode jumps directly to call 0x11b0 with the implicit '\n' argument (movsx then call) — rewrite represents this as os.put(nl) with nl='\n'. Equivalent.
[low] other: Original ends with `goto L_1200` (presumably ostream::flush tail-call) returning arg1; rewrite returns os.flush(). Equivalent.

[low] other: Original compares vtable slot at +0x30 against arg2 (an externally-passed expected pointer in the .isra.0 variant). Rewrite assumes it is &do_widen, matching the standard libstdc++ inlined endl pattern.
